source_text.text:
[PDF_PAGE 180 | score=7] 454—2 preferred study to action and disliked leaving the Tower of High Sorcery at Wayreth, he re- fused to retire while Raistlin remained a threat. He feared his decision to let Raistlin take his Test at an early age propelled him toward Evil. n the Fabulous finded and Nosy. A lovable old curmudgeon, Fizban was actu- ally an avatar of the god Paladine. In the Fourth Age, this bumbling, grandfatherly wiz- ard often appeared at just the right moment to deliver much-needed magical aid—or chicken feathers, if his spell didn’t work. Tasslehoff was always helping him find his lost hat. а D Justarius Thoughtful and Contemplative. The head of the Order of Red Robes, Justarius became leader of the Wi
[PDF_PAGE 72 | score=5] However, even without the aid of magic, heroes can offer basic medical care to the wounded immediately after a battle, using the following action: > Offer First Aid Difficulty: Average (8) Action ability: Dexterity Opposition ability: None Comments: Successfully offering first aid restores one card to the hand of a player with an injured hero. If this card restores the player's Hand of Fate to its normal size, his hero has been fully healed. Those who are still short a card (or more) require more medical atten- tion or must let time work its wonders. A hero can give first aid to himself (as above) or to a character—the latter by flipping over the top card of the Fate Deck and restoring to th
[PDF_PAGE 179 | score=5] XN is v << = Soy = 27 LEG 7 A «У >) < mained fanatically loyal to the Blue Lady until his death. This handsome but dark man's wild nature gave him berserker fury in battle. His quick temper and lust for pleasure once led him to kill the son of a noble in a fight over a woman—and led to his death when he as- saulted Laurana during the War of the Lance. Suit of Helms ‘Tough heroes and villains with a great deal of stamina appear in the Suit of Helms. 1 Ф Caramon Majere Thorough and Brave. Caramon, the burly, good-hearted Hero of the Lance, has always been slow to act, preferring to think a matter over carefully before deciding on a course of action. After the war, he retired from. adventuring 
[PDF_PAGE 147 | score=4] EE N Reds N Wy 7 ATA SS] BARES X KS = [^5 V TAN A? KEES GA OM Sool i С 4sc: The Dragon Purge Begins Using a frightening arcane ritual, Malys begins to destroy the dragons of Ansa- lon, consuming their life energy and becoming more powerful with every victory. The lands around her new home, now known as the Desolation, become burnt wastes littered with the bones of slain dragons. Other Evil wyrms learn her secrets and begin destroying Good and Evil dragons alike in their desire for personal power. Human lands become mere battlefields for these titanic clashes. Most metallic dragons vanish to the Dragon Isles. Khellendros the Blue, the famed Skie of the War of the Lance and the Blue Lady's War
[PDF_PAGE 166 | score=4] Nightlund In the time before the first Cataclysm, the Plains of Solamnia included Knightlund, named for its noble lord: Soth, Knight of the Rose. When the cursed lord was transformed into the Death Knight, a pall of undeath fell upon his land, then renamed Nightlund. Thirty years ago, the hand of Chaos spread this blight farther north and south of Soth's Dargaard Keep. Perpetual twilight now shades the entire realm. Important Holdings Dargaard Keep, Gaardlund region, city of Kalaman. Main Populace Undead, humans. Personalities Tierrel Rychner (human adult male, observant demeanor, Adven- turer); Eryn Kurras (human elder female, crafty demeanor, Adven- turer). Description Once, Dargaard Keep 
[PDF_PAGE 81 | score=3] Nd A Q Healing Effect Difficulty 1 card/point 1 point 2 cards/point 2 points 3 cards/point 3 points 4 cards/point * 4 points 5 cards/point 5 points However, the act of healing has its risks. Should the player of the healed hero happen to draw a Dragons card, a heal- Other Spell Effects. If the spell effect does not seem as easily defined as the above examples, the Narrator assigns it a difficulty rating based on his assess- ment of the spell's desired outcome. Most such magics are cast with injuri- ous or otherwise negative purposes; the table below lists some general degrees of spell effect, along with their аѕѕосі- ated numeric adjustments. ing mishap has occurred. The Dragons card indicat
[PDF_PAGE 88 | score=3] For example, an alterer could assume the shape of another individual, an ani- mal such as a horse, or even a creature like a wyvern. In addition, alterers can change the shape of someone else, though casting alteration spells on another is harder than casting upon oneself, due to the increased range. When the mystic assumes his new shape (or alters another), he must determine the new figure's Physical ability scores. The total number of points assigned to the new form's Strength, Endurance, Agility, and Dex- terity must equal the total of the altered individual's own scores. Thus, a hero with a score of 7 for each Physical abil- ity (a total of 28 points) could become a creature with scores 
[PDF_PAGE 97 | score=3] Special Abilities ‘The descriptions of offensive abilities use the term “attack” to describe their effects in combat. This is merely a term of conve- nience: Any of the abilities could be used against the heroes in a foe's counter- attack-and often are! Aeromancy See the aeromancy school of sorcery. Alteration See the alteration mystic sphere. In some cases, a limit on this ability may be indi- cated parenthetically. For example, a crea- ture with the listing "alteration (avian)" could assume only birdlike forms. Animism See the animism mystic sphere. As with alteration, an entry might note a paren- thetical limitation, like “animism (avian).” Aura An attack form or other ability described.
